summaries.Apartment:
Apartment – KB Summary (internal)

Scope  
Emails about the residence itself: move-in/move-out logistics, access (keys, fobs, digital credentials), maintenance/repairs, damage claims, amenity issues, rent‐related adjustments, or platform-mediated bookings (e.g., Airbnb host reimbursements).

Sender intent / common requests  
• Tenants: report malfunctions, flooding, noise, or ask for key replacements and appliance help.  
• Property managers / platforms: seek confirmation of damages, reimbursement approval, or status updates on work orders.  
• Vendors: coordinate repair schedules or parts delivery.

Triage & response  
1. Safety/utility outages (water leaks, lockouts) – acknowledge within 1 hr, open high-priority ticket, notify on-call maintenance.  
2. Access or amenity issues – respond < 4 hrs with instructions or ETA for fix.  
3. Financial/claim items – verify photos, invoices, lease/Airbnb ID, then forward to Finance or PM for approval; reply to sender with expected timeline.  
Log every action in the building CRM; attach relevant docs.

Style & policy cues  
Use a calm, solution-oriented tone. No PII beyond unit # and first name. Cite policy clauses if denying reimbursement. Close with clear next steps and an SLA (“We’ll update you by…”).
summaries.Lydia Chilton:
Lydia-Chilton threads are almost entirely about logistics for ongoing research collaboration—confirming, canceling, or shifting meetings, quickly flagging calendar errors, and giving brief status updates on project progress (e.g., CHI deadline, tool demos). Typical asks include: “Can we push to Monday instead?”, “Any slot after 2:30 PM Tuesday?”, “I’ll be on Zoom; can you still meet today?” or notice of illness/travel that affects attendance.

Triage & response
1. Reply same-day; scheduling uncertainty blocks her work.  
2. State clear availability windows in EDT and propose at least two concrete alternatives.  
3. Immediately update or resend the Google Calendar invite; add Zoom link if remote.  
4. Acknowledge project milestones or constraints (conference deadlines, orientation, doctor’s appointment) and adjust expectations.

Style & policies
• Keep messages brief, friendly, and solution-oriented.  
• Use email subject line continuity (“Re: <original invite>”) so auto-threading works.  
• No PII or sensitive data beyond what’s needed for scheduling.  
• Confirm any changes explicitly to avoid double bookings.
